revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 竹野神社 |画像 =[[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮249 | 神体 = | 創建 = 不明 | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 主祭神 = 天照大神 | 例祭 =9月10日 | 神事 = }} '''竹野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮にある[[神社]] == 社名 == 諸説あり『たけの』がなまって『たかの』になったとする説や竹野媛(たかぬひめ)から『たかの』とする説などがある。
== 歴史 == 創立年月は不明だが、『延喜式』の神名帳によると竹野郡「竹野神社大」と記され、竹野郡中唯一の大社の格を誇っている。室町時代には武将の保護厚く、江戸時代も宮津・出石藩主並びに久美浜代官所から崇信を受けていたとされる。現在の社殿は文政13年(1830年)に再建されたものである。
==特徴== 竹野神社は三方を塀に囲まれており、塀には全て白い二本線が入っている。天皇家にゆかりがないと白い線を入れることは出来ないとされており、天皇家にゆかりのある格式の高い神社は京丹後市の神社では竹野神社のみと言われている。<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=丹後思い出散歩あのころへ |publisher=中江忠宏 |date=2013 |page=48 |isbn=}}</ref> == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の神社]]

{{神社 | 名称 = 竹野神社 |画像 =[[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮249 | 神体 = | 創建 = 不明 | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 主祭神 = 天照大神 | 例祭 =9月10日 | 神事 = }} '''竹野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮にある[[神社]]。桜井氏の子孫が代々宮司を勤め、禰宜の桜井祐策は2016年より京丹後市議会議員でもある。(現在2期目) うた 諸説あり『たけの』がなまって『たかの』になったとする説や竹野媛(たかぬひめ)から『たかの』とする説などがある。
== 歴史 == 創立年月は不明だが、『延喜式』の神名帳によると竹野郡「竹野神社大」と記され、竹野郡中唯一の大社の格を誇っている。室町時代には武将の保護厚く、江戸時代も宮津・出石藩主並びに久美浜代官所から崇信を受けていたとされる。現在の社殿は文政13年(1830年)に再建されたものである。
==特徴== 竹野神社は三方を塀に囲まれており、塀には全て白い二本線が入っている。天皇家にゆかりがないと白い線を入れることは出来ないとされており、天皇家にゆかりのある格式の高い神社は京丹後市の神社では竹野神社のみと言われている。<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=丹後思い出散歩あのころへ |publisher=中江忠宏 |date=2013 |page=48 |isbn=}}</ref> == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の神社]]

{{神社 | 名称 = 竹野神社 |画像 =[[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|250px]] | 所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮249 | 神体 = | 創建 = 不明 | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 主祭神 = 天照大神 | 例祭 =9月10日 | 神事 = }} '''竹野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮にある[[神社]]。桜井氏の子孫が代々宮司を勤め、禰宜の桜井祐策は2016年より京丹後市議会議員でもある。(現在2期目) ==由来== 諸説あり『たけの』がなまって『たかの』になったとする説や竹野媛(たかぬひめ)から『たかの』とする説などがある。
== 歴史 == 創立年月は不明だが、『延喜式』の神名帳によると竹野郡「竹野神社大」と記され、竹野郡中唯一の大社の格を誇っている。室町時代には武将の保護厚く、江戸時代も宮津・出石藩主並びに久美浜代官所から崇信を受けていたとされる。現在の社殿は文政13年(1830年)に再建されたものである。
==特徴== 竹野神社は三方を塀に囲まれており、塀には全て白い二本線が入っている。天皇家にゆかりがないと白い線を入れることは出来ないとされており、天皇家にゆかりのある格式の高い神社は京丹後市の神社では竹野神社のみと言われている。<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=丹後思い出散歩あのころへ |publisher=中江忠宏 |date=2013 |page=48 |isbn=}}</ref> == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:京都府の神社]]

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 竹野神社 | 画像 =[[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
参道 | 所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮249 |位置 = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=竹野神社}} | 神体 = | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 祭神 = [[天照大神]] | 例祭 = [[9月10日]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[府社]] | 神事 = }} '''竹野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[式内大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。 桜井氏の子孫が代々宮司を勤め、禰宜の桜井祐策は2016年より京丹後市議会議員でもある。(現在2期目) ==社名== 諸説あり『たけの』がなまって『たかの』になったとする説や竹野媛(たかぬひめ)から『たかの』とする説などがある。 == 歴史 == 創立年月は不明だが、『延喜式』の神名帳によると竹野郡「竹野神社大」と記され、竹野郡中唯一の大社の格を誇っている。室町時代には武将の保護厚く、江戸時代も宮津・出石藩主並びに久美浜代官所から崇信を受けていたとされる。現在の社殿は文政13年(1830年)に再建されたものである。 ==特徴== 竹野神社は三方を塀に囲まれており、塀には全て白い二本線が入っている。天皇家にゆかりがないと白い線を入れることは出来ないとされており、天皇家にゆかりのある格式の高い神社は京丹後市の神社では竹野神社のみと言われている。{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=丹後思い出散歩あのころへ |publisher=中江忠宏 |date=2013 |page=48 |isbn=}} == 文化財 == === 京都府登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 竹野神社 3棟（建造物） - 1985年（昭和60年）5月15日登録。 *** 本殿 - 文政13年（1830年）造営。 *** 末社斎宮神社本殿 - 文政13年（1830年）造営。 *** 中門 - 文政年間頃の造営。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色斎明神縁起（絵画） - 江戸時代、17世紀頃の作。京都国立博物館寄託。1991年（平成3年）4月19日登録。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色等楽寺縁起（絵画） - 桃山時代、16世紀頃の作。京都国立博物館寄託。1991年（平成3年）4月19日登録。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 竹野のテンキテンキ - 1992年（平成4年）4月14日登録。 === 京丹後市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 竹野神社紙本著色斎明神縁起（絵画） - 京都府登録文化財に同じ。1986年（昭和61年）5月19日指定。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色等楽寺縁起（絵画） - 京都府登録文化財に同じ。1986年（昭和61年）5月19日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:丹後国の式内社|大たかのしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]]

{{神社 | 名称 = 竹野神社 | 画像 =[[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
参道 | 所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮249 |位置 = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=竹野神社}} | 神体 = | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = 一間社流造 | 別名 =　斎宮神社(江戸時代) | 札所等 = | 祭神 = [[天照大神]] | 例祭 = [[9月10日]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]（大）

{{神社 | 名称 = 竹野神社 | 画像 =[[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
参道 | 所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮249 |位置 = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=竹野神社}} | 神体 = | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = 一間社流造 | 別名 = 斎宮神社（江戸時代） | 札所等 = | 祭神 = [[天照大神]] | 例祭 = [[9月10日]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[府社]] | 神事 = }} '''竹野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[式内大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。 通称、斎宮(いつきのみや)と呼ばれている。桜井氏の子孫が代々宮司を勤め、現在の[[宮司]]は第59代目。[[禰宜]]の桜井祐策は2016年（平成28年）より京丹後市議会議員も務める。(現在2期目) ==社名== 社名の由来には諸説あり、「たけの」がなまって「たかの」になったとする説や、竹野媛（たかぬひめ）から「たかの」とする説などがある。 == 歴史 == 創立年月は不明だが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』によると[[竹野郡 (京都府)|竹野郡]]「竹野神社大」と記され、竹野郡中唯一の大社の格を誇っている。[[室町時代]]には武将の保護厚く、[[江戸時代]]も[[宮津藩]]・[[出石藩]]主並びに[[久美浜代官所]]から崇信を受けていたとされる。現在の社殿は文政13年（1830年）に再建されたものである。 == 特徴 == 竹野神社は三方を塀に囲まれており、塀には全て白い二本線が入っている。[[皇室|天皇家]]にゆかりがないと白い線を入れることは出来ないとされており、天皇家にゆかりのある格式の高い神社は京丹後市の神社では竹野神社のみと言われている{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=丹後思い出散歩あのころへ |publisher=中江忠宏 |date=2013 |page=48 |isbn=}}。 Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|拝殿 Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|本殿 Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|一の鳥居 Takanojinja (4).jpg|参道 == 文化財 == === 京都府登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 竹野神社 3棟（建造物） - 1985年（昭和60年）5月15日登録。 *** 本殿 - 文政13年（1830年）造営。 *** 末社斎宮神社本殿 - 文政13年（1830年）造営。 *** 中門 - 文政年間頃の造営。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色斎明神縁起（絵画） - 江戸時代、17世紀頃の作。京都国立博物館寄託。1991年（平成3年）4月19日登録。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色等楽寺縁起（絵画） - 桃山時代、16世紀頃の作。京都国立博物館寄託。1991年（平成3年）4月19日登録。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 竹野のテンキテンキ - 1992年（平成4年）4月14日登録。 === 京丹後市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 竹野神社紙本著色斎明神縁起（絵画） - 京都府登録文化財に同じ。1986年（昭和61年）5月19日指定。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色等楽寺縁起（絵画） - 京都府登録文化財に同じ。1986年（昭和61年）5月19日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:丹後国の式内社|大たかのしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]]

参道 | 所在地 = [[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮249 |位置 = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=竹野神社}} | 神体 = | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = 一間社流造 | 別名 = 斎宮神社（江戸時代） | 札所等 = | 祭神 = [[天照大神]] | 例祭 = [[9月10日]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[府社]] | 神事 = }} '''竹野神社'''（たかのじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[京丹後市]][[丹後町]]宮にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]（[[式内大社]]）で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[府社]]。 通称、斎宮(いつきのみや)と呼ばれている。桜井氏の子孫が代々宮司を勤め、現在の[[宮司]]は第59代目。[[禰宜]]の桜井祐策は2016年（平成28年）より京丹後市議会議員も務める。(現在2期目) ==社名== 社名の由来には諸説あり、「たけの」がなまって「たかの」になったとする説や、竹野媛（たかぬひめ）から「たかの」とする説などがある。 == 歴史 == 創立年月は不明だが、『[[延喜式神名帳]]』によると[[竹野郡 (京都府)|竹野郡]]「竹野神社大」と記され、竹野郡中唯一の大社の格を誇っている。[[室町時代]]には武将の保護厚く、[[江戸時代]]も[[宮津藩]]・[[出石藩]]主並びに[[久美浜代官所]]から崇信を受けていたとされる。本社、末社などは享禄3年(1530年)10月に火災で焼失したが、文政13年（1830年）3月に社殿を再建したとされる。 == 特徴 == 竹野神社は三方を塀に囲まれており、塀には全て白い二本線が入っている。[[皇室|天皇家]]にゆかりがないと白い線を入れることは出来ないとされており、天皇家にゆかりのある格式の高い神社は京丹後市の神社では竹野神社のみと言われている{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=丹後思い出散歩あのころへ |publisher=中江忠宏 |date=2013 |page=48 |isbn=}}。 Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|拝殿 Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|本殿 Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|一の鳥居 Takanojinja (4).jpg|参道 == 文化財 == === 京都府登録文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 竹野神社 3棟（建造物） - 1985年（昭和60年）5月15日登録。 *** 本殿 - 文政13年（1830年）造営。 *** 末社斎宮神社本殿 - 文政13年（1830年）造営。 *** 中門 - 文政年間頃の造営。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色斎明神縁起（絵画） - 江戸時代、17世紀頃の作。京都国立博物館寄託。1991年（平成3年）4月19日登録。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色等楽寺縁起（絵画） - 桃山時代、16世紀頃の作。京都国立博物館寄託。1991年（平成3年）4月19日登録。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 竹野のテンキテンキ - 1992年（平成4年）4月14日登録。 === 京丹後市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 竹野神社紙本著色斎明神縁起（絵画） - 京都府登録文化財に同じ。1986年（昭和61年）5月19日指定。 ** 竹野神社紙本著色等楽寺縁起（絵画） - 京都府登録文化財に同じ。1986年（昭和61年）5月19日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかのしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:京丹後市の建築物]] [[Category:京丹後市の歴史]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:丹後国の式内社|大たかのしんしや]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]]

{{draft}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Motoise shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | Image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main Deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | Annual Festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | Image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main Deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | Annual Festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

**17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:from everydodywiki with needed marking of original title]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:元伊勢]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:from everydodywiki with needed marking of original title]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:元伊勢]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

**17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]][[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}}{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]][[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}

Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}}
