Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:completion tier 1]][[Category: completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]]

Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:completion tier 1]][[Category: completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]]

Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takeno Shrine-qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]

The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]]

The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:goodbye $1]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** * **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** * **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** * **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** * **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** ** **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** ** **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** ** **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** ** **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** ** **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]]

The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}). [[ja:竹野神社]] {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a **Shinto shrine** located in **{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan**. It is listed as a **[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社}}]]**, and under the **modern shrine ranking system**, it held the status of a **{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社}}**. The shrine is commonly known as **Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)**. The **Sakurai family** has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current **chief priest being the 59th generation**. One of the shrine's **assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku**, has also served as a **Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)**. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that **"Takano"** was a corruption of **"Takeno"**, while others suggest that it derives from **Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")**. == History == The exact founding date of **Takano Shrine** is unknown. However, according to the **[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}]]**, it is recorded as **"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"**, making it the **only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine**. During the **Muromachi period**, the shrine received strong support from **local warlords**, and throughout the **Edo period**, it was **revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所}}**. The **main shrine and auxiliary shrines** were destroyed in a fire in **1530 (Kyōroku 3)**, but the shrine was rebuilt in **1830 (Bunsei 13)**{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is **surrounded on three sides by walls**, which are all marked with **two white lines**. These lines are said to be **a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family**, and **Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City** known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** ** **Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine** (Architecture) - Registered **May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)** ** **Main Hall (Honden)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** ** **Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)** – Built in **1830 (Bunsei 13)** ** **Middle Gate (Chūmon)** – Built around the **Bunsei era** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** (Painting) - **17th century (Edo period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** (Painting) - **16th century (Momoyama period)**, deposited at the **Kyoto National Museum**, registered **April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)** * **Intangible Folk Cultural Property** ** **Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)** - Registered **April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)** === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * **Tangible Cultural Properties** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** ** **Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)** - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated **May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)** == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category: Completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]][[en:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:11]]
