View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Takeno Shrine]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|12=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|12=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|12=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} [[Takeno Shrine]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|12=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|12=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|12=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} [[Takeno Shrine]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft|12=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft|12=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft|12=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} [[Takeno Shrine]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft|12=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft|12=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft|12=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} [[Takeno Shrine]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft|12=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|12=draft|12=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft|12=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|12=draft|12=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

{{afc comment|1=see [[every:Takeno Shrine]] for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} {{Promising draft}} [[Takeno Shrine]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}}

The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|竹野神社|version=88361540|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|竹野神社|version=88361540|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] }} [[every:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:25, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Takeno Shrine|Takeno Shrine]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{draft}} [[Takeno Shrine]] is a {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Motoise shrine|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Moto-Ise Shrines are [[Shinto shrines]] that used to host Amaterasu before she was moved to [[Ise Jingu]]. The first one was {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). {{infobox shrine | Name = {{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Takano-jinja (Kyotango), sandou.jpg|280px]]
Approach | location = [[Kyoto Prefecture|{{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], [[Kyōtango|{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], [[Tango Town|{{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]], Miya 249 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|44|12.07|N|135|6|45.00|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Takano Shrine}} | Sacred Object = | Founded = Unknown | Main Hall = Ichimonsha Nagare-zukuri (一間社流造) | Alternate Name = {{ill|Itsuki-no-Miya|ja|斎宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Edo period) | Pilgrimage Route = | Main deity = [[Amaterasu|{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | festival = [[September 10|{{ill|September 10|ja|9月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | type = [[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] (Major)
Former [[Prefectural Shrine|{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] | Rituals = | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Takano Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''{{ill|Takano Shrine|ja|竹野神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (たかのじんじゃ) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located in '''{{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tango Town|ja|丹後町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan'''. It is listed as a '''[[Shikinai-sha|{{ill|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', and under the '''modern shrine Ranking system''', it held the status of a '''{{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|府社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The shrine is commonly known as '''Itsuki-no-Miya (斎宮, Sacred Palace)'''. The '''Sakurai family''' has served as shrine priests for generations, with the current '''chief priest being the 59th generation'''. One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|竹野神社|version=88361540|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]]

One of the shrine's '''assistant priests, Sakurai Yūsaku''', has also served as a '''Kyōtango City Council member since 2016 (currently in his second term)'''. == Etymology == There are multiple theories regarding the origin of the shrine's name. Some believe that '''"Takano"''' was a corruption of '''"Takeno"''', while others suggest that it derives from '''Takanu-hime (竹野媛, "Princess Takanu")'''. == History == The exact founding date of '''Takano Shrine''' is unknown. However, according to the '''[[Engishiki|{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]]''', it is recorded as '''"Takano Shrine, Major (竹野神社大)"''', making it the '''only shrine in Takeno District with the status of a Grand Shrine'''. During the '''Muromachi period''', the shrine received strong support from '''local warlords''', and throughout the '''Edo period''', it was '''revered by the lords of {{ill|Miyazu Domain|ja|宮津藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Izushi Domain|ja|出石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the {{ill|Kumihama Daikansho|ja|久美浜代官所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''. The '''main shrine and auxiliary shrines''' were destroyed in a fire in '''1530 (Kyōroku 3)''', but the shrine was rebuilt in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)'''{{Cite book|title=Survey Report on Shrine Architecture|publisher=Kyōtango City Board of Education|date=2011}}. == Features == Takano Shrine is '''surrounded on three sides by walls''', which are all marked with '''two white lines'''. These lines are said to be '''a privilege reserved for shrines with ties to the Imperial Family''', and '''Takano Shrine is the only shrine in Kyōtango City''' known to have this distinction{{Cite book|title=Memories of Tango: A Walk Through the Past|author=Chūhiro Nakae|date=2013|page=48}}. Takano-jinja (Kyotango), haiden.jpg|Worship Hall (Haiden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), honden-1.jpg|Main Shrine (Honden) Takano-jinja (Kyotango), ichinotorii.jpg|First Torii Gate Takanojinja (4).jpg|Shrine Approach == Cultural Properties == === Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Three shrine buildings at Takano Shrine''' (Architecture) - Registered '''May 15, 1985 (Shōwa 60)''' ''' '''Main Hall (Honden)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Auxiliary Shrine: Itsuki-no-Miya Shrine (斎宮神社本殿)''' – Built in '''1830 (Bunsei 13)''' ''' '''Middle Gate (Chūmon)''' – Built around the '''Bunsei era''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' (Painting) - '''17th century (Edo period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' (Painting) - '''16th century (Momoyama period)''', deposited at the '''Kyoto National Museum''', registered '''April 19, 1991 (Heisei 3)''' * '''Intangible Folk Cultural Property''' ''' '''Takano no Tenki-Tenki (竹野のテンキテンキ)''' - Registered '''April 14, 1992 (Heisei 4)''' === Kyōtango City Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Tangible Cultural Properties''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of the Sai Myōjin (斎明神縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' ''' '''Takano Shrine Painted Record of Tōraku-ji (等楽寺縁起)''' - Same as the Kyoto Prefecture Registered Cultural Property, designated '''May 19, 1986 (Shōwa 61)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Takano-jinja (Kyotango)}} * ==Overview== ==Overview== ==References== {{reflist}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takano Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|竹野神社|version=88361540|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kyōtango]] [[Category:History of Kyōtango]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha in Tango Province|O-Takano Shrine]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Takeno Shrine]] [[Category:Pages from everybodywiki under right title]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:everybodywiki import successful]][[sn:Takeno Shrine]][[Category:everybodywiki import successful]] }} [[every:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:draft:Takeno Shrine]] [[ja:竹野神社]] [[sn:Takeno Shrine]] [[en:Takeno Shrine]] [[sn:akeno Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%AC%A0%E7%B8%AB%E9%82%91]]
